The Court informed Ms. Durrans that the Conservatorship fees and the Trust fees needed to be segregated and that specific amounts were needed. The Court also advised Ms. Durrans that she had to file a petition for fees in the Conservatorship matter. Counsel represented to the Court that all Parties were present and that they received notice. Counsel also clarified that the Trust is under Court supervision and presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to why the Court should approve the settlement agreement and why it is in the best interest of the Conservatee/Trustor. Discussions were held with respect to who has the legal responsibilty to sign for the Trustor or whether such signature was needed, and Attorney's fees that will be requested at a later date. The Court granted the Petition to Approve Setttlement Agreement (subject to review of the order). Ms. Stine signed the resignation in open Court. The accounting petition was continued to October 29, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court granted the Petition for Appointment of Temporary (Successor) Conservator of the Person and Estate of Ana E. Gomez which expires on November 8, 2012 (order for the same signed in open Court). Bond in the amount of $30,000.00 is required. The Court then continued the matter to May 2, 2011 for status on discovery and status on the underlying petition to invalidate.
